questions: Identify the telehealth second opinion service provided by the joint venture between Cleveland Clinic and Amwell. Based on the geographic restrictions and service tiers outlined for this program, compare the specific options available to a patient residing in Georgia versus a patient residing in Nevada. For each of these two U.S. states, specify the service level (e.g., report only or report plus visit) and the associated cost in USD. Additionally, determine if a patient located in Turkey is eligible for this service. Finally, list the three numbered steps involved in the U.S. process, starting after account creation.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic Cleveland Clinic’s ophthalmologists and optometrists have the highest training available. We provide exams, vision correction and care for many eye co
rounds.answer_a:
The telehealth second opinion service is **The Clinic by Cleveland Clinic**, a joint venture between Cleveland Clinic and Amwell, offering virtual second opinions for complex conditions like cancer and heart disease.[1][5][7]

Service tiers are **Concierge** (written report only, $1,500–$5,000 based on complexity, though exact pricing varies) and **Concierge Plus** (report + virtual visit, higher cost).[7] Availability depends on physician licensure: video visits require the specialist to be licensed in the patient's state; otherwise, only a report is provided, potentially with local physician collaboration.[5]

Search results lack state-specific licensure details for Georgia (GA) or Nevada (NV), so exact options cannot be confirmed—both likely qualify for at least Concierge (report), with Concierge Plus possible if licensed there.[5][7] Costs are not state-differentiated but tied to service level and case complexity.[7]

Patients in Turkey **are eligible**, as the program accepts patients worldwide; video visits are unavailable internationally, limiting to written report (Concierge).[1][3][7]

After account creation, the U.S. process involves three steps:
1. **Records collection**: Staff gather medical records with consent.[7]
2. **Expedited matching**: Match with a Cleveland Clinic specialist in days.[3][7]
3. **Expert second opinion**: Receive written report (± virtual visit if licensed).[3][5][7]

(Word count: 248)

## References
[1] https://www.healthcare-brew.com/stories/2024/04/19/how-cleveland-clinic-s-virtual-second-opinions-program-saves-thousands-per-patient  
[3] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions/healthcare-providers  
[5] https://www.beckershospitalreview.com/healthcare-information-technology/telehealth/how-cleveland-clinic-is-expanding-virtual-second-opinions/  
[7] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions

An incomplete answer is infinitely better than no answer.
